title: sagina apetala subsp. filicaulis
intro: Sagina apetala subsp. filicaulis, also known as lesser pearlwort or pearlwort, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the caryophyllaceae family. It is a low-growing, perennial herb with white or pinkish flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as eczema, acne, and minor cuts and scrapes. Traditionally, Sagina apetala subsp. filicaulis can be prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is sometimes used in herbal remedies for its health benefits, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
